result.markdown:
# Preface {.unnumbered}

This is the manual for **gnafr** — an R package for fast, fuzzy bulk matching of
free-text Australian addresses against the [Geocoded National Address File
(G-NAF)](https://geoscape.com.au/data/g-naf/), backed by an embedded [DuckDB](https://duckdb.org/)
database.

If you have a column of messy, human-typed addresses and want a `latitude`/`longitude`,
a standardised address, and a confidence score for each one — at the scale of a few
rows or a few million — this package (and this manual) is for you.

## Who this is for

You should already be comfortable with R and `data.table`. No DuckDB or SQL
experience is assumed — gnafr handles that internally — but [Under the
hood](03-theory.qmd) explains the SQL gnafr generates, for readers who want to
understand exactly what's happening or who need to debug an unexpected result.

## How this manual is organised

**Getting started**

- [About gnafr](01-about.qmd) — what G-NAF is, what problem gnafr solves, and how the
  package is put together.
- [Quick guide](02-quickstart.qmd) — install the package and run your first match in a
  few minutes, using a tiny in-memory database (no G-NAF download required).

**Under the hood**

- [Theory: how matching works](03-theory.qmd) — the parsing pipeline, the scoring
  formula, and the multi-path matching strategy, explained from the actual
  implementation rather than from the outside.
- [Building a good lookup table](04-build-database.qmd) — a guided walkthrough of
  [`build_gnaf_database.R`](../build_gnaf_database.R), the reference pipeline for
  turning a raw G-NAF download into a database that matches well.

**Reference**

- [Function reference](05-function-reference.qmd) — every exported function, grouped
  by task, with arguments, return values, and examples.
- [Other features](06-other-features.qmd) — the Shiny geocoder app, spatial helpers
  (`sf` + `leaflet`), synthetic test-data generation, and the match cache.
- [Troubleshooting & performance](07-troubleshooting.qmd) — common failure modes, how
  to read them, and how to keep large batch jobs fast.

::: {.callout-note}
Almost every code chunk in this manual that uses a real database is marked
`eval: false`, because it points at a multi-gigabyte G-NAF file that doesn't exist in
this build environment. Chunks that *do* run live use a tiny synthetic database
created with `gnaf_connect(":memory:")` — you'll see their actual output printed
inline, generated from the package's current source at render time.
:::

|Description             |Fast bulk address matching against the Geocoded National Address

scoring algorithm, DuckDB-backed storage for high-performance lookups
(100k+ addresses), and the ability to add custom addresses to the
matching database. |
